zh.lifeSummary: 當Graf Volkwin II von Schwalenberg und Waldeck於1125年出生在費爾斯騰圖姆 利普史瓦倫格時，他的母親Lutrud von Itter36歲，父親Widekind Ier von Schwalenberg44歲。他於大約1151年與Countess Luitgard von Reichenbach結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1188年享年64歲。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Schwalenberg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Manfred.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Schwalenberg, a place near Höxter.

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Myer.

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Pfund ‘pound’, either a nickname or one of the names chosen at random from vocabulary words in the 18th and 19th centuries by government officials at the time when surnames became compulsory.
Altered form of German   Pfund  .

variants.name: Pfund
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Claus, Erwin, Gerhard.

German: metonymic occupational name for a sealer of weights, or for a wholesale merchant, from Middle High German pfunt ‘pound’ (as a measure of weight and a unit of currency), or a nickname relating to a tax obligation.

nameMeanings.name: Waldeck
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (western Ashkenazic): habitational name from a place in Hesse, on the Eder river, probably named from Old High German wald ‘wood, forest’ + ecka, egga ‘corner’, ‘nook’.
English: variant of   Waldock  .

variants.name: Waldock
meanings: Dutch: habitational name from a place called Waldeck in South Holland or one in Germany, near Koblenz.

English (Hertfordshire): apparently a variant of the Yorkshire surname Weldrick, a habitational name from Wheldrake in East Yorkshire. The placename probably derives from Old English cweld ‘death, place where a death took place’ + ric ‘narrow strip of land, narrow road’.

variants.name: Waldecker
meanings: German: habitational name for someone from any of various places called   Waldeck  .

nameMeanings.name: Volkwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from an ancient Germanic personal name composed of folk ‘people, army’ + wini ‘friend’.

variants.name: Woolwine
meanings: Americanized form of an unidentified German surname, possibly   Volkwein  .

History: The first known bearers of the surname Woolwine are Philip Woolwine and his wife, Elizabeth, who lived in Lancaster County, PA, by 1767. In this year, their son was recorded at baptism as Johannes Wohlwein, and two years later their daughter as Barbara Wollwein. However, the surnames Wohlwein and Wollwein are not found in the German-speaking lands.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Graf Volkwin II von Schwalenberg und Waldeck was born in 1125, in Schwalenberg, Lippe, his mother, Lutrud von Itter, was 36 and his father, Widekind Ier von Schwalenberg, was 44. He married Countess Luitgard von Reichenbach about 1151. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died about 1188, at the age of 64.

Il a épousé Countess Luitgard von Reichenbach environ 1151. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1188, à l’âge de 64 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Graf Volkwin II von Schwalenberg und Waldeck(이)가 1125년 프u르스투 리프 슈월버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 어머니 Lutrud von Itter(은)는 36세였고, 그의 아버지 Widekind Ier von Schwalenberg(은)는 44세였다. 그는 대략 1151년 Countess Luitgard von Reichenbach(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1188년 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Graf Volkwin II von Schwalenberg und Waldeck родился в 1125 г. в местности щвеиленбирг, фuрстентум липп, его матери по имени Lutrud von Itter было 36 лет, а отцу, которого звали Widekind Ier von Schwalenberg, 44. Он женился на женщине по имени Countess Luitgard von Reichenbach около 1151 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер около 1188 г. в возрасте 64 лет.
